hyperedges.label: Plate-together loop (strict coursing + JIT firing + scheduler + plate time)
Re-fire priority flow (recook + fire-now + re-fire + scheduler contract)
Two-way kitchen feedback loop (loop + schedule + priority badges + line state)

nodes.label: Offline-first architecture
nodes.rationale: Offline is the default, not a fallback. Every feature must work with the network fully off for hours with no spinner-of-death because the kitchen wifi will drop mid-rush. The state layer stays connectivity-agnostic; connectivity_plus only nudges the sync engine, never blocks a write.

nodes.label: Layered architecture (Presentation/State/Domain/Data)
nodes.rationale: UI never touches Firestore or dio directly. Flow is UI -> bloc (cubit/bloc) -> Repository -> Drift (local source of truth). The sync engine moves data between Drift and Firestore in the background. Respect layer boundaries; a widget importing cloud_firestore or drift is a bug.

nodes.label: Scheduler (pure Dart)
nodes.rationale: The scheduler is pure Dart, deterministic, and unit-tested. No Flutter imports, no DateTime.now() inside it (pass now in), no Firestore calls. It is the product's IP - protect its test coverage. Schedules fire times across the whole kitchen so every dish on a ticket plates together, respecting station capacity, holdable-vs-not dishes, batching, and per-ticket SLAs.

nodes.label: Drift (local source of truth)
nodes.rationale: Drift over Isar/Hive because the domain is relational (ticket -> lines -> station; the scheduler joins across all of it) and needs complex queries + migrations. Isar is unmaintained as of 2026; Drift is the maintained SQLite-ORM default with first-class reactive streams. DAOs expose streams, never one-shot reads, for anything the UI displays.

nodes.label: bloc (flutter_bloc + Cubit) state management
nodes.rationale: Team chose bloc over Riverpod. State classes are immutable and Equatable so rebuilds are minimal and tests can assert on equality. DI via MultiRepositoryProvider + MultiBlocProvider. No business logic in widgets.

nodes.label: Sync engine (outbox pattern)
nodes.rationale: Outbox pattern; background service with zero UI coupling. Writes append to Outbox in the same Drift transaction as the local mutation (atomic). Push drains FIFO to batched Firestore writes, idempotent, exponential backoff. Pull is delta sync via updatedAt cursor.

nodes.label: Last-write-wins conflict resolution
nodes.rationale: Last-write-wins by updatedAt (server time). LWW is acceptable because a KOT is edited by one station at a time; document this assumption and revisit only if multi-device simultaneous edits become real. version increments on each write for debugging/ordering.

nodes.label: Cloud Firestore (post-MVP backend/sync)
nodes.rationale: Post-MVP - not in the build yet. Mirror of Drift; offline persistence on. Firestore offline is the transport's safety net; the outbox is ours - we don't read Firestore in widgets.

nodes.label: Vision LLM scan layer
nodes.rationale: A single vision-capable LLM call reads the photographed KOT into structured lines AND suggests a cook time per item - one multimodal request, one parseable JSON response. Vision LLM over self-hosted OCR because one call does both jobs and the model can be swapped (Claude <-> free tier) without an app release since the call goes through a backend proxy.

nodes.label: Backend proxy (key-holding)
nodes.rationale: The provider API key must NEVER ship in the Flutter app - mobile binaries are trivially unpacked, so an embedded key is a leaked key. Route the call through a thin backend proxy that holds the key server-side; the proxy can switch providers and rotate keys without an app release.

nodes.label: Fire alerts (toast + audio announce)
nodes.rationale: When the live run clock crosses a dish's fireAt, the chef must notice without looking closely - hands are full, the screen may be across the line. So two synchronized outputs fire: a large bold top toast held 5-10s and a spoken announcement. Trigger lives in the state layer, NOT the scheduler, keeping the scheduler pure. Edge-triggered and once-only via a Set of announced ids.

nodes.label: AppToast (top toast)
nodes.rationale: Toasts always pop up at the TOP via AppToast, rendered into the root overlay. Never use ScaffoldMessenger/SnackBar (those anchor to the bottom) and never hand-roll an OverlayEntry. Most-connected node in the repo.

nodes.label: AppToast.fire variant
nodes.rationale: The one deliberately big/bold/long toast variant (5-10s, large monospace, brand-orange, high-contrast, readable from across the line). A new method on AppToast, not a separate widget.

nodes.label: Announcer (on-device TTS)
nodes.rationale: Audio is a side-effecting platform concern, so wrap it behind an injected, mockable abstraction in DI. On-device only via flutter_tts + bundled chime - do not call a cloud TTS API because the kitchen wifi will drop mid-rush and the announce must still fire. NoopAnnouncer for tests/CI/muted.

nodes.label: Batching (merge identical batchable dishes)
nodes.rationale: Merge dishes with the same (stationId, dishId) whose targets fall within batchWindow (default 2 min) only if batchable. Merged dish keeps all members; target = tightest member; recompute ideal.

nodes.label: Hold-vs-late placement
nodes.rationale: Under station capacity: fits at ideal -> on time; station busy + holdable -> fire earlier into a free slot, finishes early, holdMins>0; station busy + not holdable -> fire later, plates late, lateMins>0; never fire before nowMins.

nodes.label: Lane packing (rail view)
nodes.rationale: Lane-pack each station's dishes for the rail view: first lane whose last finish <= this fire, else new lane. Guaranteed <= capacity lanes.

nodes.label: Bottleneck (station with largest late)
nodes.rationale: The scheduler surfaces the station with the largest induced lateMins - the feature's punchline (steamer can't keep up -> add a second).

nodes.label: Plate-together invariant
nodes.rationale: All dishes on one ticket finish within hold/late tolerance of each other so they plate together - back-scheduled from target = orderedAtMins + sla[type], ideal = target - cookMins, slowest dish fires first.

nodes.label: MultiKOT.jsx (React prototype)
nodes.rationale: The React prototype - behavioural source of truth for screens, states, copy, and the scheduling algorithm. When prototype and AGENTS.md conflict, the prototype wins on behaviour, this file wins on structure.

nodes.label: Result<T> / AppFailure
nodes.rationale: No exceptions across layer boundaries - repositories return Result<T>/AppFailure. Map dio/Firestore/Drift errors into typed failures at the data layer.

nodes.label: Clock abstraction
nodes.rationale: Inject into scheduler/sync; never call now() directly - keeps the scheduler and fire detector pure and testable.
